g.text: A regular Linux process. Six namespaces wall off what it sees. Cgroups cap what it can use. Overlayfs is its root filesystem. No hypervisor.
Linux kernel — enforces namespaces + cgroups + overlayfs
pulls layers • verifies sha256 • mounts overlayfs • invokes runc
kubelet (receives pod spec from API server)
cgroup ceiling: CPU ≤ 2 cores • memory ≤ 2 GB (exceed → OOM-kill, exit 137)
MNT namespace mounts overlayfs root '/' into the container — image layers ro + 1 writable upper on top:
Each surprising behaviour is a direct consequence of the model on the left.
no init system above your JVM. Killing PID 1 tears down the namespace.
• Two pods on same host can both bind :8080
separate NET namespaces, separate socket tables.
• `ps aux` sees only your container's processes
• Container 'has' 64 GB RAM even if limit is 2 GB
/proc/meminfo is NOT cgroup-aware by default. Use -XX:+UseContainerSupport (JDK 10+).
kernel OOM-killer sent SIGKILL. JVM had no chance to log or dump.
